channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Another Deadpool Secret Lair is Coming This April Fool's Day
item.content:encoded:
Wizards of the Coast has announced another Magic; The Gathering Secret Lair featuring a certain fourth-wall shattering Marvel character. For the second year in a row for April Fool's, a Deadpool Secret Lair is coming, and this time the cards are designed by the chaotic assassin himself.

This Year's Magic: The Gathering Deadpool Secret Lair

In an increasingly irreverent press release, Wizards of the Coast announced that they had hired Deadpool as a creative consultant on this year's Deadpool Secret Lair. Given the fact that Deadpool is a fourth-wall breaking chaotic character, completely self-aware of his existence as a fictional character, this is to be expected.

The release states that Deadpool personally oversaw the officially titled Secret Lair x Deadpool: I Fixed It (You're Welcome) drop, containing five "improved" cards from the game featuring Deadpool artwork, and a lot of hastily scribbled markers and crudely drawn post-it notes.

"Every Magic card is great, but some Magic cards just need a little more joie de vivre, some jeux ne se quoi, and other French words that indicate aura farming," said creative consultant All-High Grand Arbiter of Corrections Department Deadpool. "I actually AM a trading card, so I know what I'm talking about."

The five cards in this drop are as follows:

  • Deadly Dispute
  • Lightning Bolt
  • Thrill of Possibility
  • Lightning Greaves
  • Sol Ring

This Secret Lair continues the trend of Magic: The Gathering having multiple crossovers with other beloved properties. This includes Secret Lairs with Monster Hunter, Doctor Who-themed Commander Decks, and official crossover sets with Marvel's Spider-Man and the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles.

The new Deadpool Secret Lair will be available in three forms. Non-foil (MSRP $29.99), Foil (MSRP $39.99), and Pool Party Edition (MSRP $59.99), which has the cards under a different foil treatment and adorned with extra Deadpool stickers. If you want all three versions together, there is the FINAL_final_REALLYfinal_v7_USETHISONE(2)_Everything Bundle for $124.99.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler
